scope: Core vocabulary; what 'a feature' actually is vs. raw data.
key_concepts: Difference between raw data (data mentah) and features (fitur).
Attributes vs. Features: an attribute is a property of the data; a feature is an attribute transformed into a useful form for learning.
Representation (representasi): how data is structured and presented to the machine learning model so it can learn effectively.

definitions.definition: Directly collected information, not yet processed or optimized for a model.
A measurable property or characteristic of a phenomenon that has been extracted and prepared for a model.
The mathematical or structural format in which features are organized for the algorithm.

examples: Show the difference by comparing raw text (a review) vs. word frequency counts (features).
Compare an raw image (pixels) vs. edges / color histograms (features).

relations.builds_toward: ch01_s02 (how features set the learning ceiling)

exclusions: Do not discuss matrix math or specific extraction algorithms here. Keep it conceptual.
Do not mention the 'designed by human vs. learned by machine' spectrum here (that belongs to ch01_s03).
